raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California State Legislature has passed Assembly Bill 1359 to streamline the development of geothermal energy projects.",
    "Current California law treats the drilling of exploratory wells for geothermal projects as a separate project under the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA).",
    "The California Geologic Energy Management Division (CalGEM) has faced resource constraints and backlog, causing multi-year delays in geothermal exploration projects.",
    "Geothermal energy is a critical component of California's strategy to eliminate fossil fuels and mitigate climate change.",
    "AB 1359 will help unlock the potential of geothermal energy by modernizing the regulatory framework.",
    "Geothermal energy provides a constant and reliable source of clean power that complements other renewable sources like wind and solar.",
    "AB 1359 allows counties to act as the lead agency for CEQA review for geothermal projects in lieu of CalGEM.",
    "Geothermal energy offers unique advantages as a renewable resource, providing baseload power that is available 24/7.",
    "The ability to generate consistent energy makes geothermal a key player in the transition away from fossil fuels.",
    "Advanced geothermal technologies can provide reliability needed to phase out natural gas power plants.",
    "AB 1359 positions California as a leader in the deployment of advanced geothermal technologies."
  ]
}
```
